publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-05-30 02:24:07 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Journal of Applied Physics records

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection comprises records from the Journal of Applied Physics, spanning the years 1952 to 1955. During this period, Professor Earle C. Gregg served as an Assistant Editor from 1950 to 1954, while the founding editor, Elme Hutchisson, held the position from 1937 to 1953. In July 1954, Professor Hutchisson announced that the editorship of the journal had transitioned to Professor Robert Sproull, Associate Professor of Physics at Cornell University at that time. Professor Sproull served as the editor from 1954 to 1957. Professors Earle C. Gregg and Elme Hutchisson were the editors for issues published between 1952 and mid-1954, while Professor Robert Sproull took over for issues published from mid-1954 to 1955. The records are organized into two categories: accepted papers, which encompass articles and letters to the editor, and rejected, withdrawn, or transferred papers, all arranged chronologically.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Journal of Applied Physics records, #14-22-4557. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.geogname: Cornell University. School of Applied and Engineering Physics
controlaccess.subject: Physics -- Periodicals.
Physics -- History -- 20th century.
Physics
Physics--Research.

did.unittitle: Series I. Accepted Issues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Professors Earle C. Gregg and Elme Hutchisson were the editors for issues published between 1952 and mid 1954, while Professor Robert Sproull took over for issues published from mid-1954 to 1955.
